samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Implement a module for parallel to serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="ZERO">0</param>
        <param id="ONE">1</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[2:0]">4-bit counter register.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" type="sequential_logic">Valid signal register.</reg>
        <reg id="data" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit data register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <case>
            <item>Reset the counter register (cnt) when the reset signal (rst_n) is low.</item>
            <item>Set the counter register (cnt) to 0 when valid_out=1, indicating the last bit of the parallel input is output.</item>
            <item>Increment the counter register (cnt) by 1 when valid_out=0.</item>
        </case>
        <case>
            <item>Reset the data register (data) when the reset signal (rst_n) is low.</item>
            <item>Set the data register (data) to the parallel input (d) when valid_out=1, indicating the last bit of the parallel input is output.</item>
            <item>Shift the data register (data) to the left by one bit when valid_out=0.</item>
        </case>
        <case>
            <item>Set valid_out to 1 when cnt is 3.</item>
            <item>Set valid_out to 0 when cnt is not 3.</item>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>
